title: Walk symlinks when checking cached images for copied/added files
body:
If a cache is found for a copied/added file, check for symlinks
and follow the symlink to get most up to date information of
file status.

Fixes https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/1063#event-1889938970

Signed-off-by: Urvashi Mohnani <umohnani@redhat.com>

comments.body: Red Hat CI is failing, but it doesn't look related to this PR.
and how'd you manage to get the green butons, woulda thought the gofmt would have been unhappy on Go 11.   Hmmm, sometimes I just love Travis.

@TomSweeneyRedHat modifiedtimes is the goal of this PR, and I think that was moved from another section of code.  
The bug that this is trying to fix, is when a user is building with --layers, the code is supposed to figure out whether or not the content used in the Dockerfile has changed since the last run.  If the user setup a symbolic link to content, then we would check if the symbolic link changed rather then the content pointed to by the symbolic link.

@TomSweeneyRedHat yeah the goal is to compare the modified time of a file to the creation time of the cached intermediate image to determine whether to use cache or not. Initially, we were not resolving any symlinks if the file being copied/added was a symlink. This just does that as well as compare the modified time.

@umohnani8 and @rhatdan tyvm for the background info, that makes sense to me now.
LGTM
Just small nitpicks but LGTM, really nice!
